IsA: It is a marginal sea of the South Pacific.
It is classified as an interim Australian bioregion.
HasA: It has numerous islands and reefs.
It has frequent rains and tropical cyclones.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its warm climate.
It is characterized by frequent rains and tropical cyclones.
It is characterized by a subtropical climate.
UsedFor: It is used for tourism, both domestically and internationally.
LocatedIn: It is located off the northeast coast of Australia.
It is located down the Australian north-east coast to latitude 30° south.
It is located in the west by the east coast of Queensland.
It is located in the east by Vanuatu and by New Caledonia.
It is located in the northeast approximately by the southern extremity of the Solomon Islands.
It is located in the northwest, it reaches to the south coast of eastern New Guinea.
It is located in the south, merging with the Tasman Sea.
It is located in the north, merging with the Solomon Sea.
It is located in the east, merging with the Pacific Ocean.
It is located on the west, bounded by the mainland coast of Queensland.
BuiltIn: It was built between 58 million and 48 million years ago.
CausedBy: It is caused by the uplift of the Queensland continental shelf and subsidence of continental blocks.
Affects: It affects the cool waters of the Tasman Sea with warm nutrient-poor waters.
ConsistsOf: It consists of numerous islands and reefs.
It consists of the world's largest reef system, the Great Barrier Reef.
It consists of numerous coral formations.
It consists of the Great Barrier Reef, which includes approximately 2,900 individual reefs and 1000 islands.
It consists of the Chesterfield Islands and Lihou Reef, which are the largest atolls.
It consists of the Great Barrier Reef on the continental shelf.
It consists of a plateau that is host to the Coral Sea Islands Territory.
It consists of the Coral Sea Basin.
It consists of younger basins situated to the seas's east and south.
It consists of the Rennell Basin, south-west of Rennell Island.
PartOf: It is part of the South Pacific.
It is part of the Coral Sea Islands Territory.
It is part of the Coral Sea Plateau.
It is part of the Queensland Plateau.
It is part of the Coral Sea Basin.
It is part of the Rennell Basin.
It is part of the Rennell Trough.
It is part of the Rennell Island Ridge.
It is part of the South Rennell Trough.
It is part of the Santa Cruz Basin.
KnownFor: It is known for its numerous coral formations.
It is known for being the location of the Battle of the Coral Sea.
It is known for containing the world's largest reef system, the Great Barrier Reef.
It is known for its reefs and islands being particularly rich in birds and aquatic life.
It is known for being a popular tourist destination.
It is known for its warm climate.
It is known for frequent rains and tropical cyclones.
It is known for its coral formations of the Great Barrier Reef, the largest known reef system in the world.
It is known for the prevention of the Japanese sea-borne invasion of Port Moresby.
It is known for being frequently hit by tropical cyclones.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the East Australian Current.
It is associated with the Queensland continental shelf.
It is associated with the Great Dividing Range.
It is associated with seismic activity.
It is associated with the Louisiade Plateau.
It is associated with the Rennell Island Ridge.
It is associated with the Mellish Rise.
It is associated with the Santa Cruz Basin.
It is associated with the D'Entrecasteaux Basin.
It is associated with the D'Entrecasteaux Ridge.
NamedAfter: It is named after its coral formations.
OccurredIn: It occurred in May 1942 during the Battle of the Coral Sea.
It occurred between 1866–2000 with several hundred earthquakes.
It occurred on 2 April 2007 with a major earthquake followed by a tsunami.
It occurred between 1920–1999 with variations in river discharge.
It occurred between 1969 and 1997 with 80 cyclones.
It occurred between 1997 and 2005 with a decreased cyclone frequency.
It occurred between January and April with tropical cyclones.
It occurred between November–May in the areas south to 10°S with tropical cyclones.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the uplift of the Queensland continental shelf and subsidence of continental blocks.
Influenced: It influenced the cool waters of the Tasman Sea with warm nutrient-poor waters.
